text.title: SERP Intent Classification Rubric Decision Tree
text.summary: Run-or-skip gate based on preconditions, then a second-level question routing to a rule-id conclusion. Minimum-form tree for a methodology of this shape.

decision-tree.root-question: Is a live top-10 SERP snapshot available for the target query?

branch.question: Does the SERP have ≥3 dominant page types AND/OR clear SERP features?

branch.conclusion: Apply rubric: pick primary class (I/C/T/N) with ≥2 SERP signals.
Tag AMBIGUOUS_BLOCK; queue for user research before drafting.
Skip this methodology. Pull a SERP snapshot first; rubric without SERP evidence is gut-feel.

text.example: query='hubspot vs pipedrive' top10=7 comparison-blog + 2 brand + 1 review; features=featured_snippet,PAA → primary=C subtype=vs evidence=[page-type-distribution, featured_snippet] → SINGLE_BRIEF.
